spec_section_updates.section_4:
### Cycle 2

agents/cleaner.md Section 5 'Git Operations' now contains a push-gate token write block at lines 103-107. Line 103 introduces the block: 'After each successful commit (exit code 0), write the push-gate token so /push can proceed:'. Line 105 contains the full token-write algorithm (verbatim from changelog-analyst.md Phase 10): sha256(realpath(GIT_ROOT))[:16] repo-hash, JSON fields {commit_sha, branch, repo_root, session_id}, session_id collision guard. Line 107 contains the commit-failure guard: 'If the commit fails (nothing staged, or git error), do NOT write the token.'

commands/clean.md:589 reads: '2. If satisfied: Changes already committed — to publish to remote: run `/push`'. grep for '/commit' across the entire file returns zero matches.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Root cause was: agents/cleaner.md's inline git commit never wrote the push-gate token, making any subsequent /push invocation fail with token-absent rejection. Fix adds the token write immediately after the successful commit in Section 5 Git Operations (line 103). The token is now written before /clean completes, so /push finds it at the canonical path. Root cause commit c7f7afc is superseded by this change.
